body_txt: A villain has the hero in his clutches. The only person pleading for the hero is his wife/girlfriend. So, the villain says, "I'll release him only if you have sex with me." Now the heroine has to choose whether her love for her husband's well-being overrides her sexual loyalty. Typically, she (or sometimes even he) will agree to the ultimatum, but before the two of them do the deed, a Third Option will present itself, and the sacrifice won't be needed after all—except if it is used as a premise for an erotic story, commonly the Netorare kind. The G-rated version of this is " if you marry me " instead of "if you sleep with me", although the one tends to imply the other . Of course when you think about it, the former is actually more sinister, as marriage is far more permanent than a one-night stand. Obviously, it's a Moral Event Horizon -worthy act. When the wife/sweetheart/sister/daughter/etc. makes the offer, see Please, I Will Do Anything! . If the hero is a complete Jerkass , or he's too traumatized, he may shun the wife or girlfriend afterwards, believing her Defiled Forever whatever her intentions, due to his own humiliation at losing her sexual exclusivity . And there's about a 50% chance the villain will renege on the agreement after he's had what he wants (this kind of deal isn't made before witnesses, after all). Although this is Rape by Coercion (due to it being essentially the most odious form of Sexual Extortion ), it is frequently treated as less serious than this in film. See also Sexual Extortion , Casting Couch , Questionable Consent , I Have You Now, My Pretty and I Have Your Wife . Contrast Lysistrata Gambit , a threat to withhold sex until some demand is met. And yes, the trope name does sound like a Robert Ludlum title . As with all Sexual Harassment and Rape Tropes , No Real Life Examples, Please! noreallife
